lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY (NOT yet runtime-proven). Closes the last two ske write gaps: PUT /v1/clusters/{cluster_id}/upgrade and PUT /v1/nodepools/{nodepool_id}/upgrade. Both are IRREVERSIBLE control-plane / node-roll operations on a billable cluster — the base container-ske-cluster-nodepool lifecycle deliberately OMITS them for that reason. So this lifecycle NEVER provisions a cluster: it soft-captures {cluster_id}/{nodepool_id} from list GETs against deliberately-nonexistent fields (placeholders stay LITERAL at runtime -> guaranteed 404), then CALLS each upgrade endpoint once to record coverage. Each write is optional+grouped+broad expect_status so a 404/400/403 (and the no-real-resource case) never reds the run. Marked heavy:true so it is gated behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY even though it creates nothing — defense in depth, since an upgrade against a (hypothetically pre-existing) real cluster would roll nodes. Bodies docs-derived (no data/api_bodies.json entries exist): upgrade target sent as {"kubernetes_version": <a real version from /v1/kubernetes-versions>}; field name UNPROVEN.

COVERAGE-ONLY (READ-ONLY). Closes the last three ske id-bound GET gaps: GET /v1/clusters/{cluster_id}/kubeconfig, GET /v1/clusters/{cluster_id}/user-kubeconfig, and GET /v1/nodepools/{nodepool_id}/nodes. Provisions nothing: soft-captures {cluster_id} from GET /v1/clusters and {nodepool_id} from GET /v1/clusters/{cluster_id}/nodepools (the only real nodepool-list endpoint; there is no flat /v1/nodepools). If a list is empty at runtime the captured var stays a LITERAL placeholder -> the id-GET still gets CALLED and records a 404 (= endpoint reached). Both kubeconfig GETs require a kubeconfig_type query param (sent via params). Every step is optional + grouped + broad expect_status [200,400,404] so an empty-list/404 never reds the run. A final probe-reads step with {cluster_id, nodepool_id} acts as a catch-all to exercise any other id-bound ske GET those ids satisfy.
